prepared_by: loyalty and lifecycle marketing dashboard export
note: Numbers pulled from three surfaces on the same day. Aggregate headline counts differ across platforms because the birthday and nameday club, the general newsletter, and the private-event lead capture do not share a single source of truth. Deduplication is by lowercased email.

engagement_segmentation.note_on_never_engaged: Includes list imports from the 2023 Greek Festival booth clipboard capture that were added in bulk without a confirmed opt-in email.

campaigns_last_90_days.notes: Nameday club is opt-in only, list is smaller than the full newsletter
Newsletter goes to the full list, open rate lower than nameday because reach includes never-engaged contacts
Ran two days ago, redemption window still open, numbers will move
Small SMS list, opt-in captured at the host stand

private_event_lead_capture.notes: The website intake sits in a separate tool from the loyalty list. A submitter who is also on the newsletter is counted twice at headline level.

cross_platform_reconciliation_flags: Headline all-lists total (4200) is a sum of three overlapping lists and does not equal deduped unique contacts (3612).
The private-event form does not push into the loyalty tool, so a form submitter who is not already on the newsletter is invisible to campaigns unless manually added.
The 2023 festival booth import was appended to the general newsletter without a confirmation email step, and those addresses have never opened a campaign since.
Nameday and birthday clubs are subsets of the newsletter, not standalone lists, so opens on those campaigns already contribute to the newsletter open counts.
SMS opt-in list is not deduped against the email list.

list_hygiene_notes.reason_overdue: Held off during snowbird season last year to preserve reach for the seasonal offers window.
